div1.entryFree: Hostage, a pledge, a surety given, whether in persons or things: you know now your --s: your uncle's word and my firm faith, Troil. III, 2, 115. what they are that must be --s for Rome, Cor. I, 10, 29. if he stand on h. for his safety, bid him demand what pledge will please him best, Tit. IV, 4, 105. demand your --s, V, 1, 160. your --s I have, Ant. II, 6, 1. his body's h. for his return, Cymb. IV, 2, 185.
